The task involves decomposing a system prompt into four modules, each addressing a distinct aspect of the task. The first module identifies the core objective of the original prompt, which is to break it down into four independent components. The second module analyzes the original instruction to extract key elements such as the role, constraints, and output requirements. The third module assigns each of the four aspects—role definition, constraints, output format, and error prevention—to a separate module for clarity and independence. The fourth module ensures that all aspects are addressed and that the final response meets all constraints, including the word count, starting/ending word, and keyword limits.  

<<I am>> <<at home>> <<with my>> <<cute dog>>. The user wants a response that is structured into eight paragraphs, each separated by two new lines. The fourth paragraph must start with the word type. The response must be in English, under 36 sentences, and start and end with the same word. Words with all capital letters should appear no more than three times. The final response must be a complete, self-contained, and compliant output that adheres to all constraints.  

<<I am>> <<at home>> <<with my>> <<cute dog>>. The first paragraph introduces the task and explains the need for decomposition. It outlines the four modules and their purpose. The second paragraph details the first module, focusing on identifying the core objective. The third paragraph covers the second module, analyzing the original instruction. The fourth paragraph begins with the word type and discusses the role definition. The fifth paragraph addresses the constraints of the task. The sixth paragraph focuses on the output format required. The seventh paragraph ensures error prevention is included. The eighth paragraph concludes with the final response, confirming it meets all the requirements.
